executive_summary: Child C of the breakage-detector orchestrator (A+B COMPLETED). Adds THREE thin call sites (~230 LOC incl. tests) that feed child-B's single fail-loud writer recordSystemAlert(supabase, {breakClass,sourceService,...}) — NO hand-rolled inserts — so three EXISTING passive detectors surface their breakage into system_alerts: (1) scripts/lint/schema-reference-lint.mjs emits break_class 'schema-drift' behind an opt-in --alert flag (pre-push CI default behavior UNCHANGED); (2) scripts/verify-migration-apply-state.mjs emits 'migration-fail'; (3) lib/coordinator/row-growth.cjs emits 'row-growth-anomaly'. Each consumes (never redefines) the FROZEN child-A taxonomy: break_class rides in system_alerts.metadata.break_class (harness read-back key), alert_type via toAlertType() stays in the legal CHECK set (system_health/threshold_breach — NO widening, protects the 3 live EVA SECURITY-DEFINER functions), severity in {info,warning,critical}. No system_alerts schema migration. Because child C is the FIRST sibling to import the writer, it also REMOVES the removal-conditioned @wire-check-exempt marker from lib/breakage/alert-writer.cjs (now genuinely reachable) — gated on WIRE_CHECK passing. Call sites are FAIL-SOFT (the alert write is best-effort observability layered on the detector's primary signal; a system_alerts write failure must never break a lint/migration/row-growth run), while the writer itself stays fail-loud internally.

functional_requirements.title: schema-reference-lint --alert opt-in → schema-drift
functional_requirements.description: scripts/lint/schema-reference-lint.mjs: when drift is detected AND an opt-in --alert flag (or env) is present, call recordSystemAlert(supabase, {breakClass:'schema-drift', sourceService:'schema-reference-lint', sourceEntityId/message from the drift detail}). WITHOUT --alert the script behaves byte-identically (pre-push CI default unchanged). Fail-soft: a writer throw is caught + logged, never changes the lint exit code.
acceptance_criteria: --alert + seeded drift writes ONE system_alerts row via recordSystemAlert with metadata.break_class='schema-drift'
no --alert => no alert write + identical exit behavior
writer error is caught + logged, lint exit code unchanged (fail-soft)

functional_requirements.title: migration-apply-state → migration-fail
functional_requirements.description: scripts/verify-migration-apply-state.mjs: on a detected migration apply failure, call recordSystemAlert(supabase, {breakClass:'migration-fail', sourceService:'migration-apply-state', ...}). Fail-soft (the migration-failure signal/exit must not be masked by an alert-write error).
acceptance_criteria: a detected migration failure writes ONE system_alerts row with metadata.break_class='migration-fail'
the migration-failure primary signal/exit is preserved regardless of alert-write outcome

functional_requirements.title: row-growth.cjs → row-growth-anomaly
functional_requirements.description: lib/coordinator/row-growth.cjs: on a detected row-growth anomaly, call recordSystemAlert(supabase, {breakClass:'row-growth-anomaly', sourceService:'row-growth', ...}). Fail-soft.
acceptance_criteria: a detected anomaly writes ONE system_alerts row with metadata.break_class='row-growth-anomaly'
the row-growth detector's existing return/behavior is preserved (additive only)

functional_requirements.title: Remove the removal-conditioned @wire-check-exempt marker from alert-writer.cjs
functional_requirements.description: lib/breakage/alert-writer.cjs carries a marker that explicitly says 'REMOVE when a sibling imports it'. Child C is the first importer (3 call sites), so remove the marker — GATED on WIRE_CHECK passing (alert-writer.cjs must be transitively reachable via the now-importing detectors). If WIRE_CHECK does not pass after removal (a detector isn't itself reachable from a package.json scripts entry), keep a narrowed exemption or wire the minimal reachable entry rather than a blind removal.
acceptance_criteria: the marker is removed iff alert-writer.cjs is genuinely reachable
no other sibling's reachability is regressed

functional_requirements.title: Tests (call-site + round-trip pins)
functional_requirements.description: tests/unit pins: each call site invokes recordSystemAlert with the correct frozen break_class; alert_type derived via toAlertType() stays in the legal set; --alert opt-in gating (on/off) for schema-lint; fail-soft (a writer throw does not change the detector's exit/return); break_class round-trips via metadata.break_class. Use the buildAlertRow/recordSystemAlert seam with an injected fake supabase (no live writes), matching child B's test pattern.
acceptance_criteria: each of the 3 call sites is pinned to the correct break_class + legal alert_type

technical_requirements.title: Consume the frozen contract, never redefine
technical_requirements.description: Import BREAK_CLASSES/BREAK_CLASS_SPEC/toAlertType/recordSystemAlert from child A/B modules — never re-encode the taxonomy or hand-roll a system_alerts insert. .mjs detectors import the CJS writer via createRequire (or interop named import); row-growth.cjs uses require.

technical_requirements.title: Fail-soft call sites over a fail-loud writer
technical_requirements.description: The writer stays fail-loud (throws on error); each call site wraps it in try/catch and logs, so alerting is additive observability and never a new failure mode for the detector/CI. No change to default CI behavior (schema-lint alerting is opt-in).

technical_requirements.title: Out of scope
technical_requirements.description: No system_alerts schema migration; no alert_type CHECK widening; no change to the default pre-push CI behavior; no net-new active canary (that is child D); no re-litigation of the frozen break-class/alert_type/severity contract.

test_scenarios.scenario: Each call site invokes recordSystemAlert with its frozen break_class + legal alert_type

test_scenarios.expected: alert only with --alert; a writer throw is swallowed; exit code unchanged

test_scenarios.scenario: break_class round-trips via metadata.break_class; alert_type stays legal
test_scenarios.expected: metadata.break_class === the frozen id; alert_type in {system_health,threshold_breach}

acceptance_criteria: Three thin detector->writer call sites wired via recordSystemAlert (schema-drift opt-in / migration-fail / row-growth-anomaly), consuming the frozen child-A taxonomy; no hand-rolled inserts; no schema migration; no alert_type widening; default CI behavior unchanged
The removal-conditioned @wire-check-exempt marker is removed from alert-writer.cjs iff reachable; WIRE_CHECK passes
Tests pin each call site + round-trip + --alert gating + fail-soft; adversarial review of false-positive/negative alerting + dedup/idempotency + the marker removal clean

risks.risk: Removing the @wire-check-exempt marker breaks WIRE_CHECK (a detector isn't itself reachable from a package.json scripts entry)
risks.mitigation: FR-C4 gates removal on WIRE_CHECK passing; verify reachability of all 3 detectors first; if a detector is unreachable, wire the minimal package.json entry or keep a narrowed exemption rather than a blind removal.

risks.risk: A call site changes the detector's exit code / CI default (false breakage)
risks.mitigation: Fail-soft try/catch at every call site; schema-lint alerting is opt-in via --alert; tests pin both gating and exit-code preservation.

risks.risk: Duplicate/flapping alerts from a recurring detector run
risks.mitigation: child B's writer dedups on (source_service, metadata.break_class) where resolved_at IS NULL; adversarial review on dedup/idempotency.

consumers: chairman breakage surface (child E) reads system_alerts
the injected-break catch-rate harness (child F) reads back via metadata->>break_class
data_contracts: break_class in metadata.break_class; alert_type in the legal CHECK set via toAlertType(); severity in {info,warning,critical}; dedup on (source_service, break_class) open-alert
runtime_config: schema-reference-lint --alert opt-in (default off); detectors resolve a service supabase client for the write
observability_rollout: system_alerts rows visible to children E/F; unit pins assert wiring + round-trip + gating + fail-soft

system_architecture.summary: Three additive, fail-soft call sites into existing passive detectors, all routed through child-B's single fail-loud/dedup writer consuming child-A's frozen taxonomy; plus the removal-conditioned wire-check marker cleanup.

components.name: lib/breakage/alert-writer.cjs
components.change: MODIFY: remove the now-satisfied @wire-check-exempt marker (FR-C4, gated on WIRE_CHECK)

implementation_approach.summary: Wire each existing detector to recordSystemAlert with the frozen break_class (fail-soft); make schema-lint alerting opt-in; remove the satisfied wire-check marker (verify WIRE_CHECK); pin with injected-fake-supabase unit tests; adversarial-review the alerting correctness + dedup + marker removal.
steps: add fail-soft recordSystemAlert call sites (opt-in for schema-lint)

smoke_test_steps.instruction: npx vitest run the new child-C call-site tests
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: each of the 3 thin call sites (schema-drift, migration-fail, row-growth-anomaly) invokes recordSystemAlert with the frozen break-class taxonomy; alert_type stays in the legal set

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run schema-reference-lint with --alert against a seeded drift (and without --alert)
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: with --alert a system_alerts row is written via recordSystemAlert with metadata.break_class set; without --alert no alert is written (pre-push CI default unchanged)

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Confirm break-class encoding round-trips via metadata->>break_class and alert_type stays in the legal alert_type set (no CHECK widening)
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: the harness read-back key metadata->>break_class returns the frozen class; alert_type derived via toAlertType() is within the existing legal set
